 {"id":"rich_rust-xl5","title":"Add Integration Tests for Table Rendering","description":"## Objective
Integration tests verifying complete Table rendering pipeline.

## Test Cases Required

### Basic Tables
- [ ] Single row, single column
- [ ] Multiple rows, single column
- [ ] Single row, multiple columns
- [ ] Full table (multiple rows and columns)

### Table Features
- [ ] With title
- [ ] With caption
- [ ] With header row
- [ ] With footer row
- [ ] Row separators (show_lines=true)
- [ ] No edge borders (show_edge=false)

### Column Sizing
- [ ] Auto-sizing columns
- [ ] Fixed width columns
- [ ] Min/max width constraints
- [ ] Ratio-based expansion
- [ ] Table wider than content (expand=true)
- [ ] Table narrower than content (collapse)

### Cell Features
- [ ] Multi-line cells
- [ ] Styled cell content
- [ ] Vertical alignment (top, middle, bottom)
- [ ] Horizontal alignment (left, center, right)

### Box Styles
- [ ] ASCII box
- [ ] ROUNDED box
- [ ] DOUBLE box
- [ ] HEAVY box
- [ ] safe_box fallback

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Empty table
- [ ] Empty cells
- [ ] Very narrow width
- [ ] Very wide content
- [ ] Wide characters in cells

## Files to Create/Modify
- tests/table_integration.rs (new file)","acceptance_criteria":"All table features tested. Rendered output matches expected.","notes":"## Logging Requirements

Table integration tests must have DETAILED logging for debugging:

1. **Table structure**: Columns, rows, options
2. **Width calculation**: 
   - Available width
   - Minimum widths per column
   - Maximum widths per column
   - Calculated widths
   - Collapse/expand operations
3. **Row rendering**: Content per cell, alignment applied
4. **Border drawing**: Characters used, positions

This is critical because table layout bugs are hard to diagnose without logs.

Example:
```rust
#[test]
fn test_table_width_calculation() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    let span = info_span!("table_width_calc", width = 50);
    let _enter = span.enter();
    
    let table = Table::new()
        .add_column(Column::new("A").width(20))
        .add_column(Column::new("B"))
        .add_column(Column::new("C"));
    
    debug!(columns = ?table.columns, "Table columns");
    
    let widths = table.calculate_widths(50);
    debug!(result_widths = ?widths, "Calculated widths");
    
    let total: usize = widths.iter().sum();
    info!(total_width = total, available = 50, "Width distribution");
    
    assert!(total <= 50, "Total width exceeds available");
}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:12.280758298-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T01:53:36.563572723-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T01:53:36.563572723-05:00","close_reason":"tests/e2e_table.rs already contains 37 comprehensive integration tests covering all required table features: basic tables, column sizing, cell features, box styles, and edge cases.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-xl5","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-33q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:25.821352752-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-xz9","title":"Add Unit Tests for Markup Parser","description":"## Objective
Comprehensive unit test coverage for src/markup/mod.rs.

## Test Cases Required

### Basic Parsing
- [ ] Plain text (no markup)
- [ ] Single tag: [bold]text[/]
- [ ] Self-closing style application
- [ ] Named close: [/bold]

### Nested Tags
- [ ] [bold][italic]text[/][/]
- [ ] [bold][italic]text[/italic][/bold]
- [ ] Deep nesting (3+ levels)
- [ ] Sibling tags

### Style Inheritance
- [ ] Inner tag inherits outer
- [ ] Inner color overrides outer
- [ ] Attributes combine

### Escaping
- [ ] \[ produces [
- [ ] \] produces ]
- [ ] \\ produces \
- [ ] Mixed escaped and tags

### Error Cases
- [ ] Unclosed tag at EOF
- [ ] Unmatched close tag
- [ ] Invalid style in tag
- [ ] Empty tag []
- [ ] Malformed tag [bold
- [ ] No panic on any input

### Complex Cases
- [ ] [link=URL]text[/]
- [ ] [bold red on blue]text[/]
- [ ] Multiple tags same line
- [ ] Tags with whitespace

## Files to Create/Modify
- src/markup/mod.rs (expand tests section)","acceptance_criteria":"100% test coverage for markup parser. No panics on any input.","notes":"## Logging Requirements

Markup parser tests must log the parsing process:

1. **Input**: The markup string being parsed
2. **Tokens**: Each token as it's recognized
3. **Stack**: Tag stack state during parsing
4. **Output**: Final segments with styles

Example:
```rust
#[test]
fn test_nested_markup() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    
    let input = "[bold][red]hello[/red][/bold]";
    info!(input = %input, "Parsing markup");
    
    let segments = render(input);
    
    for (i, seg) in segments.iter().enumerate() {
        debug!(
            index = i,
            text = %seg.text,
            style = ?seg.style,
            "Segment"
        );
    }
    
    assert_eq!(segments.len(), 1);
    let style = segments[0].style.as_ref().unwrap();
    assert!(style.attributes.is_set(Attributes::BOLD));
    
    info!("Nested markup PASSED");
}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:11.457599519-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T00:01:59.465607497-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T00:01:59.465607497-05:00","close_reason":"Added 30 unit tests to markup parser (16→46 total). Covers: deep nesting (3-4 levels), sibling tags, style combination, escaping edge cases, empty/malformed input, unicode/emoji, no-panic verification, link params, handler syntax, hex colors. All 46 tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-xz9","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-33q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:25.769330717-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
-{"id":"rich_rust-y3v","title":"Fix Table FootRow separator using wrong box chars when show_lines enabled","description":"## Bug Description
When a Table has `show_lines(true)` and `show_footer(true)`, the separator between the last body row and the footer row incorrectly uses `row` box characters instead of `foot_row` box characters.

## Reproduction
See `tests/repro_table_foot_row.rs` for a reproduction test case.

## Expected Behavior
The separator between the last body row and footer should use the `foot_row` characters from the BoxChars (e.g., `*` in the test case).

## Actual Behavior
The separator uses `row` characters (e.g., `-`) instead.

## Location
The bug is likely in `src/renderables/table.rs` around lines 1026-1038 where body row separators and footer separators are rendered.

## Analysis
- Line 1026-1030: Row separator for body rows (uses RowLevel::Row)
- Line 1034-1038: Footer separator (uses RowLevel::FootRow)

The condition at line 1026 `(!is_last)` correctly prevents Row separator for the last body row. However, the FootRow separator rendered at line 1036 appears to be using the wrong RowLevel somehow.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T21:05:54.150518262-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T02:08:22.583189078Z"}

The condition at line 1026 `(!is_last)` correctly prevents Row separator for the last body row. However, the FootRow separator rendered at line 1036 appears to be using the wrong RowLevel somehow.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T21:05:54.150518262-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T02:09:37.757300445Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T02:09:37.757233218Z","close_reason":"Bug was fixed in previous commit. Added repro test to verify fix."}
 {"id":"rich_rust-yfr","title":"Validate JSON Module (feature-gated)","description":"Validate the JSON module (requires "json" feature flag) against Python Rich JSON behavior.

FEATURE FLAG: json (requires serde_json crate)

COMPONENTS TO VALIDATE:
1. Json struct construction
2. JsonTheme customization
3. Pretty-printing with indentation
4. Syntax coloring for different value types

VALIDATION CHECKLIST:
- [ ] Json::new() accepts JSON string or value
- [ ] JsonTheme struct with color fields for each type
- [ ] String values styled (quotes, content)
- [ ] Number values styled
- [ ] Boolean values styled (true/false keywords)
- [ ] Null values styled
- [ ] Object braces and commas
- [ ] Array brackets and commas
- [ ] Key styling in objects
- [ ] Proper indentation levels
- [ ] Sort keys option (if supported)
- [ ] Compact vs pretty mode (if supported)

EDGE CASES:
- [ ] Empty object {}
- [ ] Empty array []
- [ ] Deeply nested structures
- [ ] Long strings
- [ ] Unicode in keys and values
- [ ] Very large numbers
- [ ] Invalid JSON input handling (JsonError)

FILES TO EXAMINE:
- src/renderables/json.rs
- Python rich/json.py for reference","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:14:08.780183637-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:50:36.072869041-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:50:36.072869041-05:00","close_reason":"Validated JSON Module - FULLY COMPLIANT. Implementation includes: Json struct with new()/from_str(), JsonTheme with all 7 color fields (key, string, number, boolean, null, bracket, punctuation), configurable indent/sort_keys/highlight options, proper string escaping, JsonError for invalid input. 24 unit tests cover all functionality including edge cases (empty objects/arrays, nested structures, custom themes). Library compiles with --features json.","labels":["feature-gated","validation"]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-yqr","title":"Create E2E Test: Panel and Nested Renderables","description":"## Objective
E2E tests for Panel rendering and nested renderable combinations.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Basic Panel
```rust
#[test]
fn test_e2e_basic_panel() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    
    let panel = Panel::new("Hello World")
        .title("Greeting")
        .border_style(BoxStyle::ROUNDED);
    
    let console = Console::new().width(40);
    let output = console.render_to_string(&panel);
    
    debug!(output = %output, "Rendered panel");
    
    // Verify rounded corners
    assert!(output.contains('╭'), "Missing top-left corner");
    assert!(output.contains('╮'), "Missing top-right corner");
    assert!(output.contains("Greeting"), "Missing title");
    assert!(output.contains("Hello World"), "Missing content");
    
    info!("Panel test PASSED");
}
```

### Scenario 2: Panel with Styled Content
- Content with markup: Panel::new("[bold]Important[/]")
- Verify: Markup rendered inside panel

### Scenario 3: Nested Panels
- Panel containing Panel
- Verify: Proper nesting, no border collision

### Scenario 4: Table in Panel
- Panel containing Table
- Verify: Width constraints propagate correctly

### Scenario 5: All Box Styles
- ASCII, ROUNDED, SQUARE, DOUBLE, HEAVY, MINIMAL
- Verify: Correct characters for each

### Scenario 6: Title Alignment
- Left, Center, Right title alignment
- Verify: Title position correct

## Logging Requirements
- Panel dimensions calculated
- Content measurement
- Border character selection
- Nested content rendering trace

## Files to Create
- tests/e2e_panel.rs
- tests/golden/panel_*.txt","acceptance_criteria":"All panel scenarios pass. Nested renderables work correctly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:45:02.652711143-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T12:39:45.448364848-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T12:39:45.448364848-05:00","close_reason":"Completed 38 Panel E2E tests covering all scenarios: basic panels, styled content, box styles, title alignment, width/padding, wide characters, table in panel, and edge cases","labels":["e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-yqr","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-6d6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:45:12.927059312-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-yqr","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-4ot","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:54.052551956-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-yz6","title":"EPIC: Performance Optimization","description":"## Objective
Optimize rich_rust for production performance, focusing on hot paths identified through profiling.

## Background
Terminal rendering can be performance-critical in CLI tools. Key optimizations include:
1. Caching frequently computed values
2. Minimizing allocations in hot paths
3. Efficient string building for ANSI output
4. Lazy evaluation where possible

## Scope

### Caching (High Priority)
- LRU cache for color parsing
- Cell width caching for repeated strings
- Style ANSI code caching

### Allocation Reduction
- Reuse buffers in rendering loop
- SmallVec for small collections
- String interning for common values

### Algorithm Optimization
- Table width calculation
- Text wrapping
- Segment merging

### Benchmarks
- Establish baseline benchmarks
- Measure improvement after each optimization
- Prevent regressions

## Success Criteria
- Measurable improvement in benchmark suite
- No performance regressions
- Acceptable memory footprint","acceptance_criteria":"Benchmarks show improvement. No regressions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:26.604866419-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T02:24:42.402424418-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T02:24:42.402424418-05:00","close_reason":"Core performance optimizations complete: (1) LRU cache for color parsing with 1024 entries; (2) cell_len caching with 1024 entries; (3) Style parse caching with 512 entries; (4) Benchmark suite in benches/render_bench.rs. Core caching shows significant performance improvement. Remaining optimizations (string allocation, style ANSI caching) can proceed as follow-up tasks."}
